content: <@&1166257943742787615> apologies for the ping <:bow:1356053037159944473> i would like to get an answer for this since i plan to make some for my friend groupsoon if possible?

Thank you so much for your enthusiasm! We have an open attitude toward fan-made merchandise. For fan-created items, as long as the quantity is within 200 units, simply let us know and ensure proper respect and protection of the IP; commercialization is allowed.
However, items made using official assets are not permitted for commercial use. That said, giving them as gifts among friends is of course totally fine.
✅「Answered」Question: Policy on fan-made/unofficial items
Thank you for the answer!!

content:
Interacting with an ultra space slot with the cursor to consult the card in it, might lead to that card being stuck on screen and not allowing to interact with anything, essentially soft locking.

The Softlock is fixed by restarting the application, the battle progress is saved

Have encountered this bug twice (including the one in the recording)

content: The new autoscroll during story is very annoying. I often have to check the log because it goes by too fast in the beginning. It also doesn't allow to click and make the rest of the text appear when it slows down near the end, making it take longer to get through if I have read at the correct speed. I prefer how it was before, with you needing to click to forward any text longer than the textbox. Would you consider going back to that?
Thank you for the feedback—we’ll look into this!
✅「Answered」Story autoscroll

Great idea! We also have lots of ideas for skins. However, this would consume a significant amount of our already limited production capacity. That said, as the art team continues to be rebuilt, there may be opportunities in the future!
✅「Answered」Fun Skin suggestions?

@everyone 

I’m truly grateful for everyone’s passionate discussion—and even for considering our business balance. It means a lot to me.

We fully understand that triple reruns can feel stressful, but please remember our original intention: to help each of you get the one character you want more quickly by offering more options—since everyone’s wishlist priorities can be very different. 

Also, because we keep releasing new characters, if we don’t adjust the number of reruns, they will never catch up with new releases, which is clearly not ideal.

Regarding advance notices, we want to keep a healthy balance between surprise and planning. Our ideal goal is to announce about 21 days in advance. However, due to production constraints and recent delays, we haven’t always been able to meet that target. We’ll continue working on this.

We have indeed been experimenting with adjustments on the monetization side lately. Thanks to your support, the game’s monetization has improved significantly, allowing us to slightly expand the team and create more interesting content for everyone. That said, please remember we haven’t abandoned other areas either—some future plans have already been shared in previous dev logs for your reference.

Thank you again for the enthusiastic discussion! I’m proud that we have such a rational and passionate community.
Please always remember: for some people, making games is a way to make money. For us, making money is a way to make games.
✅「Answered」Triple Reruns in the Future
triple reruns are great
Knowing in advance is key tho

<:wish:1356084702468899117> thanks for the response

will there be anything done similar for event reruns as well? some of the players would like to get certain SR event wheels of destiny but we're kind of just stuck waiting until the event actually reruns... 

maybe have the event SR also be in the event shop of the current rerun event or maybe add them to the standard wheel of destiny pool like the Awakeners in those events?
After a character joins the standard pool, we also plan to add their SR Wheels of Destiny to the shop for exchange to address this issue. However, this will take some time.

I just answered this question in another thread, and I’d like to add a bit more here. 

In the global version, there are many design decisions we had to make under constraints—for example, various negative effects and several key balance missteps that are now difficult to adjust. Some descriptions are overly verbose without delivering their intended design impact. These issues would negatively affect operations in any new region going forward.

We want to take this opportunity—building on the global version and the new JP experience—to correct those issues. The good design ideas from JP will, in turn, be adopted back into the global version. This process will also make our game design team stronger by exposing us to a completely different set of possibilities, helping us get closer to the “truth” of our game’s design. So as to bring a better gameplay experience to players in all regions.

Thank you for your concern about this topic!
✅「Answered」Separation of GLB and JP
🤔踹两jio

content:
I’ve previously shared that the team will expand appropriately, which will improve our overall capacity. None of this would be possible without the Japan release. 

Staffing-wise, combat design does add new workload, but that mainly affects game designers; for the global version, the primary bottlenecks are art and narrative design.

On the asset side, the JP region largely reuses prior global updates, so there’s minimal pressure there. Even if there are skins, new special Wheels, voiceovers, or other UX changes, we plan to eventually carry them over to the global version as well. 

The additional revenue from operating in two regions will be reinvested into R&D as much as possible, and by sharing development assets, this should ultimately strengthen our capabilities.

I’ve already shared a lot in my previous dev logs about what we plan to build next. However, because these features require substantial development resources and are brand-new challenges for us, I won’t commit to specific timelines right now—we want to ensure they’re genuinely fun before releasing them.

Thank you for caring about the game and the team. We’ll keep working hard!

Anyway, will hope for the best, thx for your time.

✅「Answered」Add „seen all“ button for Archive

We completely understand the issue you encountered. Taunt and Fortress are not stackable, but playing them consecutively can still be meaningful in certain situations. For example, if another character gains Taunt and other buffs via an ultimate or Wheels but you don’t want that Taunt, you can refresh it by applying a copied Taunt to override the unwanted state. Similarly, if you are Vulnerable for some reason, you may need to use Fortress once to cancel the Vulnerable first, and then use it again to actually gain Fortress.
Therefore, we don’t consider this a problem. Please pay attention to the scenarios in which you need to use this card.
